parsed_review.summary: CP00-762 (Migration Platform Permission Audit Service, Risk B, 40 units, RP25.P02.M04.S21-RP25.P02.M05.S30) is a descriptor-only pack that honestly does not claim production_ready, runtime_ready, or enterprise trust (manifest production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, status=in_progress; construction-inspection production_ready=false, descriptor_verified=false; gate_outcome=passed_pending_hardened_review). Implementation (registry.js/service.js/validators.js) faithfully follows the already-closed CP756-CP761 descriptor pattern: CP762 deliverable_counts sum to 40 and match the baseline distribution exactly, the M04 S21-30 (SERVICE_REVIEW_ROWS.slice(20,30)) and M05 S01-30 (full SERVICE_REVIEW_ROWS) row mapping is correct and non-overlapping with prior packs, and the hardened coverage/descriptor validators enforce descriptor_only, no sensitive payloads, no source-system/import runtime, no permission-decision/cross-tenant exposure, and no audit-event bodies. Tests assert CP762 coverage, a runtime-closed descriptor (section_count 2, all *_runtime_opened=false, hermes emits_runtime_receipt=false, claude read_only=true), and latest contract projection. The generated contract shows validation.valid=true with cp762 coverage and descriptor valid=true and empty errors. Command-evidence reports exit_code 0 for all 10 required validation commands plus node --check/--test and build, and git diff --check is clean; no_real_data=true and writes_product_state=false. Authority boundaries hold: Claude is read-only (Read/Grep/Glob) and non-final (claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false), human approval is required for runtime opening, and Hermes patterns are used only for descriptor review-packet/receipt shapes, not product runtime. CP178+ hardening is satisfied because the current claude-review-result.json is the pre-review PENDING placeholder (valid_review_receipts=[], 0-byte stdout) and is explicitly marked as not counted as closeout evidence, so no auth-failure/0-byte/malformed/prose/tool-shaped output is being treated as a valid receipt. The pending adjudication.md and construction-inspection.json are the expected pre-receipt state. One cosmetic P3 noted in the review-harness scope metadata; it does not affect closeout. No P0/P1/P2 issues found.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json dual-classifies handoff doc and pack artifact dir as both untracked-implementation and unrelated-preserved
findings.details: docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md and docs/closeout-packs/cp00-762/ appear in both untracked_implementation_files (lines 200-203) and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 205-222). The cp00-762/ directory is in fact this pack's own artifacts (not unrelated), and new-session-handoff.md is a stale 2026-06-08 onboarding doc unrelated to CP00-762. This is cosmetic harness bookkeeping: the safety-critical field active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty ([]), so no in-scope pack implementation file was excluded from review, and the worktree_transition_policy correctly preserves all dirty files.
findings.recommendation: Optionally refine the scope generator so pack-owned artifact paths (docs/closeout-packs/cp00-762/) are not also tagged as unrelated_dirty_files_preserved, to reduce ambiguity in future receipts. No change required for CP00-762 closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: Acknowledged as cosmetic metadata only; non-blocking. active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted=[] confirms full in-scope coverage, so this does not block CP00-762 pack or goal closeout.
